name: Dorset (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the sands of the Dorset coast

description: A gold (yellow) field with a red cross fimbriated white extending to the edges (the Cross of Saint Wite).
history: The 'Dorset Cross' or 'Cross of St Wite' was the winning entry in a 2008 public competition organised by Dorset County Council and BBC Radio Solent; designed by Stephen Coombs.

features.symbolism: the cross of St Wite (or Candida), Dorset's patron saint
